Affiliate Traffic:
Affiliate Marketing
About Affiliate Marketing:
System Description: Affiliate marketing is one of the most
powerful forms of traffic generation. It’s ideal for any business
because affiliates are only paid based on their performance. Es-
sentially, affiliates offer your products or services on their web-
sites, blogs and/or email lists. You pay commissions on the sales
or leads that are generated from their efforts. You can also in-
centivize your affiliates with prizes and extra cash.
Required Skills: An affiliate manager needs to have great
interpersonal and written communication skills. They need
to be organized and motivated to research, recruit and train
your affiliates.
System Checklist:
Step 1: ResearchAffiliates Using Google, Videos 1 and 2
1. Go to www.google.com.
2. Type in: related:(url) to see all sites related to the
URL that you are researching.
3. Type in: allintitle:(keyword) to see all sites with a
specific keyword in the title.
4. Type in: allinurl:(keyword) to see all sites with a
specific keyword in the URL.
5. Type in: link:(URL) to see all sites that are linked to
Weekly Action Plan:
Day 1 Task Checklist:
Find new affiliates using
Google.
Find new affiliates using Af-
filiate Elite.
Keep track of all new affili-
ates on an Excel spreadsheet.
Day 2 Task Checklist:
Find the week’s top promoters
in your niche on ClickBank.
Research affiliates using the
Way Back Machine.
Keep track of all new affiliates
on an Excel spreadsheet.
Day 3 Task Checklist:
Research affiliates using
DomainTools.com.
Research affiliates using
PayDotCom.com.

-- 1 of 3 --

a specific URL.
Note: This information is valuable as you are look-
ing for competitors and other people with products
in your similar niche.
Step 2: Research Affiliates via Affiliate Elite, Video 3
1. Go to www.AffiliateElite.com.
2. Click on “Find Profitable Products to Promote” to
find merchant IDs. You can choose to look for top
promoters in Clickbank.com, PayDotCom.com and
CJ.com.
3. Copy the Merchant ID for the top products.
4. Go back to the initial screen, select option four in
the drop down box and type in the merchant ID.
This will pull up some of the top promoters for that
merchant ID. Then look for contact information.
Step 3: Clickbank Research, Video 4
1. Click on marketplace and then choose a category.
2. Click on top products. This is great for researching
for two reasons: you can see what types of products
are selling well in your respective niche and you
can find the top marketers who are selling the most
products and contact them for possible joint ven-
tures. They may be open to promoting for you.
Step 4: The Way Back Machine, Video 5
1. Go to www.archive.org.
2. Enter a domain that you want to research in the
Way Back Machine toolbar.
3. Click on previous dates to look at the page. This
helps you identify their marketing skills, and helps
System Checklist:	Weekly Action Plan:
Affiliate Research:
www.DomainTools.com
www.AffiliateElite.com
www.PayDotCom.com
www.ClickBank.com
www.Archive.org
www.Alexa.com
www.Google.com
Resources:
Keep track of all new affiliates
on an Excel spreadsheet.
Day 4 Task Checklist:
Research affiliates using
Google.
Keep track of all new affiliates
on an Excel spreadsheet.
Day 5 Task Checklist:
Contact the new affiliates
you found to see if they will
promote for you.

-- 2 of 3 --

you estimate the size of their email list.
Step 5: Research in DomainTools.com
1. Go to www.DomainTools.com.
2. Enter a domain to find contact information for
the owner. This could be a competitor or someone
that you don’t know. You are looking for contact
information for potential affiliates or joint venture
partners.
3. The information will be outlined with possible contact
information including an email and phone number.
Step 6: Research in PayDotCom.com
1. Go to www.PayDotCom.com.
2. Click on marketplace.
3. Click a category related to your niche. For example,
business to business.
4. Search the websites of the top products. This will
give you a good idea of what products are hot and
who their creators are.
5. Go to the individual websites and look for contact
information.
Step 7: Research in Google
1. Go to www.Google.com.
2. Type in the keywords that you want to research.
3. Look at both natural sites that come up and paid
sites on the right hand side.
4. Go to the sites and look for areas where the product
owner is collecting a name and email address.
5. Look for contact information on the page, and con-
tact that potential affiliate or joint venture partner.
System Checklist:	Quick Tip:
The trick is to be patient.
Some affiliates like to stay
“underground” and only
promote products that they
find. They don’t want to be
contacted. When you are re-
searching affiliates, you need
to act like you are an investi-
gative reporter. It takes time,
but is definitely worth it.

-- 3 of 3 --
